title: Bankruptcy proceedings commenced
description: Tokyo District Court commenced bankruptcy proceedings for MtGox Co., Ltd. on April 24, 2014.

title: Chapter 11 filing and collapse
description: FTX and affiliated debtors filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on November 11, 2022.

title: Binance launched
description: Binance began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: CCAA protection and insolvency crisis
description: Quadriga sought and received CCAA protection on February 5, 2019 after the platform had shut down on January 28, 2019.

title: FinCEN penalties and U.S. enforcement action
description: FinCEN assessed penalties against BTC-e on July 26, 2017 as U.S. enforcement action escalated.

title: January 2019 exchange hack
description: Official liquidation materials describe a serious January 2019 hack that caused severe operational and reputational damage.
notes: The official source confirms the hack occurred in January 2019, but not the precise day.

title: Liquidators appointed and trading suspended
description: Cryptopia reached an operational end state associated with hack.

title: Operations wound down
description: Global exchange brand from the broader Bittrex lineage, later wound down after regulatory pressure and liquidation proceedings.

title: Operations ceased
description: Centralized exchange founded in 2018 that ceased CEX operations in May 2023 after deteriorating operating conditions and earlier disruption tied to an investigation.

title: Ownership changed through acquisition
description: Poloniex underwent an acquisition-related ownership change.
notes: Ownership transition only; exchange continued operating.

title: Vaultoro launched
description: Vaultoro began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Platform migration underway
description: Official messaging described a temporary migration with customer assets stated to remain safe.
notes: Official site showed a migration/update notice in April 2026 with customer assets stated to remain safe. Launch date uses the May 2015 public launch reference for the trading service; company/about material also claims operations since 2014.

title: Cryptsy launched
description: Cryptsy began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Cryptsy reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Later reporting places the exchange launch in May 2013. The operational end marker uses the January 2016 disclosure of hidden losses and withdrawal failure.

title: BitGrail launched
description: BitGrail began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to year level.

title: Operations ceased
description: BitGrail ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Founding year is treated as 2017 from retrospective reporting. Death marker uses the Florence court order to cease operations on 2018-05-03; the primary collapse trigger remained the February 2018 Nano loss.

title: Operations ceased
description: Livecoin ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses the specific April 2015 operating-since statement from the shutdown coverage. Some secondary profiles cite earlier founding dates; these are left in notes rather than used as the operational launch marker.

title: Operations ceased
description: MapleChange ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public reporting conflicts on whether the terminal crisis is better characterized as a hack or an exit scam.

title: Cobinhood platform went live
description: Cobinhood began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange platform.
notes: HEI uses the 2017-12-18 live-platform report as the launch marker.

title: Cobinhood was moved to dead-side handling after shutdown notice
description: Cobinhood reached its terminal HEI operating state after the 2020-01-10 shutdown and account-balance audit notice.
notes: The notice described an audit period and later reopening for users to retrieve funds; HEI uses the first shutdown notice as the terminal marker while keeping the cause unknown.

title: Altsbit launched
description: Altsbit began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Operations ceased
description: Altsbit ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public coverage indicates the venue was only a few months old by February 2020. The operational end marker uses the published refund deadline in May 2020.

title: Operations wound down
description: WEX reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses the September 2017 rebrand/launch announcement. Death marker uses the late-November 2018 domain-loss point as an operational end marker; exact final shutdown timing remains contested.

title: Operations wound down
description: BX Thailand reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.

title: Operations interrupted
description: COSS went offline and then entered an acquisition/relaunch process, leaving long-term status ambiguous.
notes: COSS was taken offline on 2020-01-07 and later described acquisition negotiations and a relaunch plan. Because the record is too ambiguous for a clean dead-side classification, it is moved to inactive pending deeper verification.

title: Vircurex launched
description: Vircurex began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Vircurex reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses repeated October 2011 references. Operational end marker uses the March 2014 withdrawal freeze and account-freeze period, which followed earlier hacks and a reserve crisis.

title: Operations ceased
description: CoinsMarkets ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch remains approximate because strong primary founding records are still missing. Operational end marker uses the point when users widely reported trading and withdrawals disabled on 2018-01-05.

title: MintPal launched
description: MintPal began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations ceased
description: MintPal ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses a specific February 2014 launch reference. Death marker uses 2014-10-14 when Moolah announced MintPal had been shut down while preparing bankruptcy; later fallout continued after that date.

title: Vault of Satoshi launched
description: Vault of Satoshi began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations wound down
description: Vault of Satoshi reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses October 2013 reporting on the exchange going live. Closure date and reason come from the January 2015 shutdown announcement specifying a voluntary end rather than insolvency or theft.

title: Poloniex spins out from Circle
description: Poloniex leadership spun the exchange out of Circle into Polo Digital Assets, Ltd., continuing operations outside Circle.
notes: Ownership / corporate structure change; not a death-side terminal event.

title: Trading and wallet use suspended
description: LocalBitcoins suspended trading and normal wallet use on 2023-02-16, leaving withdrawals only afterward.
notes: Closure was announced on 2023-02-09; this event uses the effective service-stop date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Coinsetter acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Cavirtex acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
description: BitBay announced on 2021-11-08 that the exchange brand had changed to Zonda, with legacy BitBay platform URLs later redirecting to Zonda domains.
notes: Further downstream rebranding to zondacrypto should be handled as successor lineage, not silent overwrite.

title: BITFRONT service terminated
description: BITFRONT's closure notice wound down service in stages and ended withdrawals and remaining customer access on 2023-03-31.
notes: The initial shutdown announcement was published on 2022-11-28.

title: OPNX ceased operations
description: OPNX told users to withdraw funds and export historical data by 2024-02-14 as part of its February 2024 shutdown.
notes: Shutdown date is supported by strong reporting; launch lineage traces to the March 2023 CoinFLEX-to-OPNX rebrand.

title: Youbit halted trading and filed for bankruptcy
description: Youbit stopped trading and entered bankruptcy proceedings after disclosing a second 2017 hack that reduced customer balances.
notes: The retained marker combines the service halt and bankruptcy filing date used in same-day reporting.

title: Gatecoin ordered to wind up and cease operations
description: Gatecoin ceased operations after a Hong Kong court granted a winding-up order against the company, leading to compulsory liquidation.
notes: Liquidators were formally appointed on 2019-03-20; HEI keeps 2019-03-13 as the operational stop and terminal marker.

title: Koinex permanently disabled trading
description: Koinex permanently disabled digital-asset trading on all platforms on 2019-06-27 and moved to withdrawals-only wind-down.
notes: The later withdrawal deadline of 2019-07-15 is kept as supporting context, not the entity death marker.

title: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor customer base and domains
description: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor's customer base and domains in October 2021 and began migrating or closing legacy Coinfloor accounts.
notes: Further lineage work should attach the successor relationship once CoinCorner is modeled in canonical data.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the CleverCoin acquisition on 2016-06-28 and said customer accounts would be automatically transferred the next day.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-06-29 transfer completion date.

title: Coinnest shutdown process began
description: Coinnest posted a closure notice saying it was no longer operating as of Tuesday and set an April 30 deadline for withdrawals.
notes: The retained event date follows the effective service-stop marker referenced in reporting on April 18–19, 2019.

title: Liqui announced shutdown and 30 day withdrawal window
description: Liqui announced it was shutting down on 2019-01-28 and told users they had 30 days to withdraw funds.
notes: Shutdown rationale quoted in reporting focused on insufficient liquidity and lack of economic justification for continued service.

title: Nova ceased trading and API access
description: Nova said trading would cease and the API would be deactivated on 2019-10-07 as part of its shutdown schedule.
notes: Withdrawals were left open until 2019-10-25, but the operational end marker used here is the trading/API stop date.

title: Interim receiver secured Einstein Exchange
description: The Supreme Court of British Columbia appointed an interim receiver on 2019-11-01, which then secured the premises and shut down platform access.
notes: BCSC said platform counsel had told regulators on 2019-10-31 that the business planned to shut down within 30 to 60 days due to a lack of profit.

title: The Rock Trading interrupted platform operations
description: The Rock Trading said it had to interrupt operation of its platform because of difficulties encountered in liquidity management.
notes: The operational stop preceded judicial liquidation and insolvency proceedings in Milan.

title: VirWoX permanently closed its service
description: VirWoX notified users in late 2019 that the service would permanently close on 2020-01-06 and account access would end that day.
notes: The retained date follows the stated end-of-service date rather than the earlier announcement date.

title: Bitcurex went offline after reported external interference
description: Bitcurex reported asset losses and went offline in October 2016.
notes: Followed by a prosecutor investigation in 2017.

title: Tradehill halted trading again
description: Tradehill halted trading in August 2013 because of banking and regulatory issues.
notes: Earlier 2012 shutdown remains contextual evidence.

title: Coin.mx operators were arrested and charged
description: U.S. authorities charged Coin.mx operators with running an unlawful bitcoin exchange and money-laundering scheme.
notes: Enforcement-side operational end marker.

title: Intersango closed its remaining GBP market
description: Intersango progressively shut markets during 2012 and closed its remaining GBP market in December.
notes: Uses the final GBP closure date.

title: Binance Uganda fully shut down
description: Binance Uganda announced a phased wind-down and fully shut down on 2020-11-28.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Mt. Gox acquired Bitomat
description: After Bitomat lost wallet data and customer balances, Mt. Gox acquired the service and migrated users in August 2011.
notes: Successor is Mt. Gox.

title: Cointrader closed after alleged hack
description: Cointrader said it had closed its doors effective immediately after a debilitating hack.
notes: Immediate shutdown notice.

title: Yunbi announced closure of trading operations
description: Yunbi said it would close trading during the 2017 China crackdown on exchanges.
notes: China crackdown closure.

title: Binance Jersey fully shut down
description: Binance Jersey wound down through November 2020 and fully shut down on 2020-11-30.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Binance Singapore ended DPT services
description: Binance.sg shut down by 2022-02-13 after the operator withdrew its licensing effort.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: ACH and wire transfers halted
description: CampBX halted ACH and wire transfers after its bank decided not to work with bitcoin companies because of regulatory uncertainty.
notes: Earlier operational stress marker, not the final terminal event.

title: Georgia cease and desist became final
description: The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance said an order to cease and desist against Bulbul Investments LLC doing business as CampBX became final on 2018-07-25.
notes: Used as the terminal marker for public-ready handling.

title: CryptoBridge shutdown announced
description: CryptoBridge announced it would close because of market conditions and increasing regulation.
notes: Deposits were to close on 2019-12-03.

title: CryptoBridge services terminated
description: CryptoBridge said all services and servers would terminate after 2019-12-15.
notes: HEI uses 2019-12-15 as the terminal marker.

title: Coinroom entered the KNF warning list
description: Coinroom was added to the Polish KNF public warning list over suspected unlicensed payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, but not the final terminal event.

title: Coinroom shut down
description: Users reported receiving account-termination notices and Coinroom ceased operating in early April 2019.
notes: Cause remains conservative because later reporting mixes regulatory and fund-loss frames.

title: Bitsane withdrawals began failing
description: Users reported that Bitsane withdrawals began failing in May 2019, with support citing temporary technical reasons before the exchange later disappeared.
notes: Start-of-failure marker uses month-level dating from aligned reporting.

title: Bitsane website and social accounts disappeared
description: By 2019-06-17, the Bitsane website and social media accounts had disappeared after weeks of withdrawal failures, and public reporting later treated the case as an apparent exit scam.
notes: HEI uses the 2019-06-17 disappearance date as the terminal marker while preserving the later 2019-06-27 public inactive / scam-style reporting in evidence.

title: Cold wallet hack
description: Bter said 7,170 BTC were stolen from its cold wallet and the platform was temporarily shut down.
notes: Major historical incident, but not the final terminal event.

title: Bter users migrated to Gate.io
description: Gate.io announced support for BTER users to move funds to gate.io, reflecting the completed brand transition.
notes: HEI uses 2017-12-31 as the conservative rebrand marker.

title: Abucoins entered the KNF warning list
description: The Polish KNF added Abucoins sp. z o.o. to its public warnings list over suspected unauthorized payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, not the final terminal event.

title: Abucoins closed down
description: By 2019-02-26, Abucoins was publicly marked closed and inactive.
notes: Uses the 2019-02-26 closure marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Cryptonit marked closed
description: By 2019-04-01, Cryptonit was publicly described as having closed down operations.
notes: Conservative terminal marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Operations closed
description: A public update stated that Cryptonit had closed down operations, referencing an announcement email to customers.
notes: HEI uses 2019-04-01 as the terminal date.

title: C-CEX became inaccessible
description: By 2019-06-27, public exchange-status sources reported that the website was not accessible.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: C-CEX marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C-CEX as dead or closed by 2019-06-27.
notes: HEI uses 2019-06-27 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Coinbase announced the transition away from Coinbase Pro
description: Coinbase announced it would replace Coinbase Pro with Coinbase Advanced on Coinbase.
notes: Announcement month used conservatively from official help text.

title: Coinbase Pro discontinued
description: Coinbase documentation states Coinbase Pro was discontinued in December 2023 and replaced by Coinbase Advanced.
notes: HEI uses 2023-12-31 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: BTC38 site failed to load
description: By 2019-06-12, public exchange-status sources reported that the BTC38 website kept loading without completing.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: BTC38 marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BTC38 as dead by 2019-06-12.
notes: HEI uses 2019-06-12 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Bleutrade announced move to Portugal
description: Bleutrade announced a move to Portugal and closure of its Malta office.
notes: Useful lineage context, not the terminal event.

title: Bleutrade marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources reported the website was inaccessible on 2023-03-23 and marked Bleutrade as dead.
notes: HEI uses 2023-03-23 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: LakeBTC site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-07-15, public exchange-status sources reported that the LakeBTC website was unsuccessful to access.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: LakeBTC marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked LakeBTC as dead on 2021-07-15.
notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Mercatox closure update posted
description: Public exchange-status sources stated that Mercatox had closed down operations and would cease responding to user inquiries on 2025-04-01.
notes: Conservative announcement marker from the public closure update.

title: Mercatox support ended
description: Public closure text stated Mercatox would cease responding to user inquiries on 2025-04-01.
notes: HEI uses 2025-04-01 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ACX site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-07-15, public exchange-status sources reported that the ACX website was not accessible.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: ACX marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked ACX as dead on 2021-07-15.
notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Cryptox site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-11-11, public exchange-status sources reported that the Cryptox website was not accessible and there had been no prior maintenance notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: Cryptox marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Cryptox as dead on 2021-11-11.
notes: HEI uses 2021-11-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: C-Trade platform became unusable
description: By 2023-01-11, public exchange-status sources stated that the C-Trade platform was not possible to use.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: C-Trade marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C-Trade as dead on 2023-01-11 after the exchange posted a notice about building a brand new platform.
notes: HEI uses 2023-01-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: NIX-E showed terminal warning signs
description: By 2019-07-18, public exchange-status sources said NIX-E no longer reported volume to major sites, had an invalid site certificate, and appeared to have stale trading activity.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: NIX-E marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked NIX-E as dead by 2019-07-18.
notes: HEI uses 2019-07-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ZT.com site became inaccessible
description: By 2024-09-18, public exchange-status sources reported that the zt.com website was unsuccessful to access and there had been no prior maintenance or migration notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: ZT.com Exchange marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked ZT.com Exchange as dead by 2024-09-18, and CoinMarketCap later showed withdrawals and trading suspended.
notes: HEI uses 2024-09-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: AAX suspended withdrawals and operations
description: AAX suspended activity including withdrawals amid a delayed system upgrade and partner failure.
notes: Initial terminal arc marker.

title: AAX marked dead
description: By 2023-01-11, public exchange-status sources said the website was inaccessible and marked AAX as dead.
notes: HEI uses 2023-01-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: InstaSwap site became inaccessible
description: By 2024-09-18, public exchange-status sources reported that the InstaSwap website was unsuccessful to access and there had been no prior maintenance or migration notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: InstaSwap marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked InstaSwap as dead by 2024-09-18.
notes: HEI uses 2024-09-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Uniswap was publicly launched on Ethereum mainnet
description: Uniswap was publicly announced and deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2018-11-02.
notes: Entity-level launch marker for the protocol family.

title: Uniswap v2 mainnet launch
description: Uniswap v2, the second major protocol version, was deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2020-05-18.
notes: Major protocol-version milestone under the same umbrella entity.

title: Uniswap v3 mainnet launch
description: Uniswap v3, the third major protocol version, was deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2021-05-05.
notes: Major protocol-version milestone under the same umbrella entity.

title: SushiSwap launched in August 2020
description: Sushi launched in August 2020 as a decentralized exchange protocol, with official materials describing the project as created in late August 2020.
notes: Month-level conservative launch marker.

title: Sushi announced multichain suite expansion
description: Sushi announced the launch of its suite of DeFi products across major chains, reinforcing its multichain operating model.
notes: Meaningful expansion milestone for the umbrella DEX entity.

title: Sushi introduced Route Processor 6
description: Sushi introduced RP6 as the latest upgrade to its routing stack, showing continued protocol development and execution-layer improvement.
notes: Development-signal milestone retained under event_type=other.

title: PancakeSwap launched in 2020
description: Official PancakeSwap materials describe the protocol as launched in 2020.
notes: Year-level conservative launch marker.

title: PancakeSwap v3 announced in April 2023
description: Official PancakeSwap documentation states that V3 for Exchange and Farms was announced in April 2023.
notes: Month-level conservative marker based on official docs wording.

title: PancakeSwap introduced Infinity
description: PancakeSwap introduced Infinity as a major protocol evolution with a more modular architecture.
notes: Development-signal milestone retained under event_type=other.

title: Bisq went into production
description: Bisq documentation states that the network went into production on 2016-04-19 after two years of development and testing.
notes: Entity-level launch marker.

title: Bisq 2 launched in 2024
description: Current Bisq materials describe Bisq 2 as the next-generation platform launched in 2024.
notes: Year-level conservative marker for the current-generation platform.

title: NLExch closure statement surfaced
description: A public update stated that NLExch had closed down and cited Dutch registration requirements and related operating costs.
notes: Conservative announcement marker from public exchange-status coverage.

title: NLExch marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked NLExch as closed and moved it to the exchange graveyard on 2020-09-01.
notes: HEI uses 2020-09-01 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Rippex closure notice was displayed
description: Public exchange-status coverage states that Rippex displayed a closure notice on its website in Portuguese.
notes: Announcement timing remains uncertain in this pass.

title: Rippex marked permanently closed
description: Public exchange-status sources describe Rippex as permanently closed.
notes: Terminal timing remains conservative because a precise effective date is not fixed in this pass.

title: Bitmaszyna site became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that attempts to access bitmaszyna.pl on 2021-07-15 were unsuccessful and that there had been no prior maintenance or migration notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: Bitmaszyna marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Bitmaszyna as dead on 2021-07-15.
notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: RCMP seized TradeOgre and its cryptoassets
description: RCMP Federal Policing – Eastern Region announced that it seized more than 56 million dollars in cryptocurrency from TradeOgre and that the platform had contravened Canadian laws and regulations.
notes: Primary terminal enforcement event.

title: TradeOgre was dismantled and effectively shut down
description: Public reporting and the RCMP statement describe the 2025-09-18 action as the dismantling of TradeOgre, ending exchange operations.
notes: HEI uses 2025-09-18 as the terminal marker.

title: German authorities seized eXch infrastructure
description: The Frankfurt public prosecutor's cybercrime unit and the BKA seized the Germany-based server infrastructure of eXch and confiscated cryptocurrency assets, with support from the Dutch FIOD.
notes: Primary enforcement action.

title: eXch was shut down as a swap service
description: Authorities stated that the seizure action shut down the eXch platform before the operators' announced 2025-05-01 closure date.
notes: HEI uses 2025-04-30 as the terminal marker.

title: DSX bankruptcy filing was reported
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-01-12 that DSX had filed for bankruptcy and displayed an administrator notice from DSX Global (UK) Limited.
notes: Primary public bankruptcy marker.

title: DSX was treated as dead after the bankruptcy filing
description: Public exchange-status sources treated DSX as effectively dead after the 2021-01-12 bankruptcy update.
notes: HEI uses 2021-01-12 as the terminal marker.

title: Titanex was treated as missing from operation
description: Public exchange-status coverage classified Titanex as having disappeared from normal operation.
notes: Conservative terminal lead-in based on graveyard-style handling.

title: Titanex was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Titanex as dead on 2023-01-03.
notes: HEI uses 2023-01-03 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: KKCoin was treated as shut down under regulation-linked handling
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-01-01 that KKCoin had closed down and linked the terminal handling to regulation-related restrictions.
notes: Primary public terminal update.

title: KKCoin was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated KKCoin as dead on 2021-01-01.
notes: HEI uses 2021-01-01 as the terminal marker.

title: Ternion was treated as closed for business reasons
description: Public exchange-status coverage treated Ternion as closed on 2021-01-01 and classified the terminal handling under business reasons.
notes: Primary public terminal update.

title: Ternion was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Ternion as dead on 2021-01-01.
notes: HEI uses 2021-01-01 as the terminal marker.

title: Allbit closed all exchange services
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that Allbit closed down all exchange services with effect as of 2021-03-22.
notes: Primary public terminal update.

title: Allbit was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Allbit as dead on 2021-03-22.
notes: HEI uses 2021-03-22 as the terminal marker.

title: Eterbase suffered a major hot-wallet hack
description: Public reporting stated that Eterbase lost more than five million dollars from compromised hot wallets and entered maintenance mode.
notes: Primary crisis event.

title: Eterbase paused operations and was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-04-22 that Eterbase had paused operations and was moved to dead-side handling.
notes: HEI uses 2021-04-22 as the terminal marker.

title: CoinExchange.io announced closure
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CoinExchange.io closed down on 2019-10-15 pursuant to a board decision made on 2019-10-01 and described the decision as purely business driven.
notes: Primary public terminal update.

title: CoinExchange.io was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated CoinExchange.io as dead on 2019-10-15.
notes: HEI uses 2019-10-15 as the terminal marker.

title: Coinsuper was marked dead after 2021 financial troubles
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that financial troubles persisted through 2021 and later resulted in the firm closing business, after which the platform was marked dead.
notes: Primary public terminal update.

title: Coinsuper was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Coinsuper as dead in late 2021 under business-reasons handling.
notes: HEI uses 2021-12-02 as the public dead-side marker in this pass.

title: MercuriEx closure update surfaced
description: Public exchange-status coverage preserved a 2020-11-18 update stating that MercuriEx was closing down.
notes: Retained as the announcement marker rather than the terminal death-date marker.

title: MercuriEx was treated as dead by year-end 2020
description: Public dead-side coverage placed MercuriEx in the 2020-12-31 business-reasons cohort.
notes: HEI uses the graveyard terminal marker rather than the earlier announcement date.

title: Bitlish published a final closure notice
description: Publicly preserved closure text stated that Bitlish would stop providing services to all customers on 2020-03-30 and required users to withdraw remaining assets by the same date.
notes: The same closure notice also said that technical problems affected parts of the service.

title: Bitlish stopped providing services and was treated as dead
description: Cryptowisser marked Bitlish as dead on 2020-03-30 after the exchange closed down.
notes: HEI uses 2020-03-30 as the terminal marker in this pass.

title: Nominex announced closure of its centralized exchange
description: Nominex informed users by email that it was closing its centralized exchange for economic reasons and advised users to withdraw funds as soon as possible.
notes: The closure notice said the wind-down process could take up to four months.

title: Nominex was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Nominex as dead on 2024-12-18 after the closure announcement for its centralized exchange.
notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than the later stages of the operational wind-down.

title: Coindirect announced closure of its retail service
description: Coindirect announced that it was closing down its retail service after five years and would focus on its B2B business instead.
notes: The notice instructed users to withdraw crypto and fiat assets before 2023-01-24.

title: Coindirect retail exchange access terminated and was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Coindirect as dead-side on 2023-01-24 after access to retail accounts was set to be disabled the same day.
notes: HEI uses the retail-service termination date as the terminal marker.

title: Hoo Exchange was reported as failing to process withdrawals
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that numerous independent sources reported that Hoo Exchange was not processing withdrawals and that accounts were being blocked without explanation.
notes: Cryptowisser also noted that its researchers received no response when contacting the exchange for clarification.

title: Hoo Exchange was marked scam and moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Hoo Exchange as a scam and moved it to graveyard handling on 2022-08-17.
notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than later suspended-activity warnings.

title: BiteBTC faced widespread scam and blocked-withdrawal accusations
description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded extensive user accusations describing BiteBTC as a scam and reporting serious platform trust issues.
notes: The review also stated that the original site behavior had become unreliable and confusing.

title: BiteBTC was marked dead and moved to scam-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BiteBTC as dead on 2019-11-01 after concluding that it was widely acknowledged as a scam.
notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than later domain behavior.

title: Livecoin reported a major platform attack
description: Public reporting stated that Livecoin lost control over parts of its infrastructure during a late-December 2020 attack and the platform became unsafe to use.
notes: Cryptowisser later referenced the 23-24 December 2020 attack window when moving the exchange to dead-side handling.

title: Livecoin was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Livecoin as dead on 2020-12-27 after the hacker attack, before the later formal closure notice.
notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than the later business-closure announcement date.

title: Trade Satoshi announced closure
description: Trade Satoshi announced that deposits would no longer be processed and instructed users to complete withdrawals before all services terminated on 2020-03-01 00:00 UTC.
notes: Public reporting tied the announcement to economic infeasibility and a final withdrawal deadline.

title: Trade Satoshi terminated services and was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources recorded that Trade Satoshi closed down on 2020-03-01 and moved it to dead-side handling.
notes: HEI uses the stated 2020-03-01 00:00 UTC service termination point as the terminal marker.

title: IDAX users lost withdrawal access amid CEO disappearance
description: Public reporting stated that withdrawals were suspended and that the exchange's CEO had gone missing, leaving users unable to access funds.
notes: This is the crisis phase that later matured into full dead-side handling.

title: IDAX was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked IDAX inactive and effectively dead by 2020-03-31 after months of suspended deposits and withdrawals and unresolved CEO-disappearance claims.
notes: HEI uses the 2020-03-31 public dead-side marker rather than the earlier first-warning date.

title: Bololex Exchange website became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that the Bololex Exchange website could not be accessed on 2024-09-18 and that there had been no preceding notice of maintenance, migration, or recovery plans.
notes: The inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

title: Bololex Exchange was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Bololex Exchange dead on 2024-09-18 after the site became inaccessible and no continuity notice was found.
notes: HEI uses the 2024-09-18 public dead-side marker as the terminal date.

title: Bitzlato was required to shut down after founder arrest
description: Public reporting stated that the arrest of Bitzlato's founder and concurrent enforcement action resulted in the platform being required to shut down.
notes: The same-day enforcement action is the immediate operational stop marker.

title: Bitzlato was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Bitzlato dead on 2023-01-18 after the founder arrest and shutdown-triggering enforcement action.
notes: HEI uses the 2023-01-18 enforcement-day dead-side marker as the terminal date.

title: BQT platform showed no sign of active operations
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that the BQT website was up but the exchange could not be accessed and the firm's last visible social activity had been earlier in 2022.
notes: This inaccessible-but-still-online state is the immediate trigger for the later dead-side handling.

title: BQT was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BQT dead on 2022-12-02 after the platform showed no operational signs and could not be accessed as an exchange.
notes: HEI uses the 2022-12-02 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Bgogo announced closure of its centralized exchange and transition to Gogoswap
description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded that Bgogo decided to close the centralized part of its platform and transition the decentralized side into Gogoswap, instructing users to withdraw assets from the centralized exchange.
notes: The update stated that the old centralized exchange would stop trading before 2021-08-31.

title: Bgogo was moved to dead-side handling after rebranding
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Bgogo dead on 2021-08-31 after the centralized platform shut down and the decentralized component was rebranded into Gogoswap.
notes: HEI uses the rebranding-day dead-side update as the terminal marker for the centralized exchange.

title: BiKi announced closure due to regulatory reasons
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that BiKi decided to close down its operations due to regulatory reasons and announced this on its website.
notes: The same-day update is the key public closure marker.

title: BiKi was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BiKi dead on 2021-11-30 after the regulatory-reasons closure announcement.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-11-30 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Exnomy published service termination notice
description: Public exchange-status coverage captured a notice stating that EXNOMY services were terminated on July 8, 2024, that trading was no longer possible, and that asset withdrawals had to be requested by email.
notes: The notice was later observed when the website became inaccessible in September 2024.

title: Exnomy was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Exnomy as dead based on the posted termination notice stating that services had ended on 2024-07-08.
notes: HEI uses the stated service-termination date rather than the later September 2024 observation date.

title: CoinBene website became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CoinBene's website could not be reached on 2022-02-01 and that there had been no preceding messages about maintenance, migration, or any replacement site.
notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

title: CoinBene was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked CoinBene dead on 2022-02-01 after the site became inaccessible and no continuity notice was found.
notes: HEI uses the 2022-02-01 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Bitholic announced transition to Bithumb Singapore
description: Public reporting stated that Bitholic changed its name to Bithumb Singapore as part of Bithumb's expansion into Singapore.
notes: The same-day reporting framed the change as a name change and acquisition-linked platform transition.

title: Bitholic was moved to dead-side handling after rebranding
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Bitholic inactive on 2019-08-08 after it was acquired and thereafter known as Bithumb Singapore.
notes: HEI uses the rebranding-day inactive marker as the terminal date for Bitholic.

title: Coineal announced final shutdown timeline
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-11-01 that Coineal had already stopped registration and identity verification for new mainland China users in 2019 and would officially cease operations on 2021-11-15.
notes: HEI uses the posted 2021-11-01 update as the public shutdown-announcement marker.

title: Coineal officially ceased operations and was treated as dead
description: Public exchange-status sources treated Coineal as dead based on the posted statement that the platform would officially cease operations on 2021-11-15.
notes: HEI uses the stated official cessation date rather than the earlier update date.

title: CoinEgg website became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that CoinEgg's website could not be reached on 2021-07-15 and that there had been no preceding messages on system maintenance, replacement sites, or similar continuity plans.
notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

title: CoinEgg was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked CoinEgg dead on 2021-07-15 after the site became inaccessible and no continuity notice was found.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-07-15 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: UniDAX website became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that UniDAX could not be accessed on 2021-03-11 and that there was no accompanying information on system maintenance or similar continuity messaging.
notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

title: UniDAX was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked UniDAX dead on 2021-03-11 after the site became inaccessible and no continuity notice was found.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-03-11 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Rfinex website became inaccessible
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that Rfinex could not be accessed on 2021-04-10 and that there was no information on system maintenance or anything similar.
notes: This inaccessible-site observation is the immediate trigger for later dead-side handling.

title: Rfinex was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Rfinex dead on 2021-04-10 after the site became inaccessible and no continuity notice was found.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-04-10 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Vebitcoin announced cessation of all activities
description: Public coverage recorded Vebitcoin's notice that it had ceased all activities after financial strain and would update clients as soon as possible.
notes: The shutdown notice is the key public closure marker used by HEI.

title: Vebitcoin was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Vebitcoin dead on 2021-04-26 after the closure notice on its domain.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-04-26 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: C2CX announced that trading operations had ceased
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-05-10 that C2CX had closed down and reproduced a site notice indicating that trading operations had ceased.
notes: The public update and reproduced site notice are the key shutdown marker used by HEI.

title: C2CX was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C2CX dead on 2021-05-10 after the exchange announced that it had closed down.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-05-10 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Coinzo was reported to have shut down amid regulatory pressure
description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-10-11 that Coinzo seemed to have closed down its operations and that Turkish media had linked the shutdown to increased regulatory pressure from Turkish authorities.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-10-11 public update as the shutdown announcement marker.

title: Coinzo was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Coinzo dead on 2021-10-11 after reports that the exchange had shut down.
notes: HEI uses the 2021-10-11 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

title: Gatecoin announced winding-up and immediate cessation of operations
description: Public reporting reproduced Gatecoin's notice that a Hong Kong court had granted a winding-up order and that operations ceased immediately.
notes: Some secondary coverage appeared after the 2019-03-13 order date; HEI uses the order and immediate cessation date.

title: BitMarket announced closure due to loss of liquidity
description: BitMarket posted a notice stating that, due to loss of liquidity, Bitmarket.pl/net was forced to cease operations from 2019-07-08.
notes: The platform notice is quoted by multiple public sources.

title: BitMarket was moved to dead-side handling after liquidity failure
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BitMarket inactive after the platform announced that it had ceased operations because of liquidity loss.
notes: HEI uses the closure-notice date as the terminal marker.

title: Cobinhood announced shutdown and account-balance audit
description: Cobinhood announced that the exchange would shut down for an account-balance audit from 2020-01-10 to 2020-02-09 and warned users not to deposit assets during that period.
notes: The announcement said the exchange would reopen on 2020-02-10 for users to retrieve funds, but public reporting treated the situation as troubled and ambiguous.

title: OpenLedger DEX closure notice was published
description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded an OpenLedger DEX closure notice on 2020-04-25 stating that the exchange would close down as of 2020-05-15.
notes: HEI keeps the update date separate from the later terminal close-down date.

title: OpenLedger DEX closed down
description: Public exchange-status sources state that OpenLedger DEX closed down as of 2020-05-15 and is no longer operational.
notes: HEI uses the 2020-05-15 close-down date as the terminal marker.

title: ANXPRO announced it would not resume exchange services
description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded an ANXPRO website message stating that the exchange had decided not to resume digital assets exchange services.
notes: The public message is treated as the shutdown / non-resumption announcement marker.

title: ANXPRO was moved to dead-side handling
description: Public exchange-status sources treated ANXPRO as inactive after the exchange stated that it would not resume digital-asset exchange services.
notes: HEI uses the 2020-09-01 non-resumption message as the terminal marker.
